description:
🕒 17:30–20:30

Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno Ecotourism Association: https://hanno-tourism.jp/hanno-eco/tour/ec-morimana-satoyamahotaru/

This firefly observation takes place at "Aojishi Valley" (アオジシ谷), where environmental work has been done to protect fireflies. It's an evening-to-night tour where you can learn thoroughly about Genji fireflies and Heike fireflies—covering the secrets of their glow, their ecology, history and culture, and unexpected human connections.

【Fee】
Adults: ¥6,000 / Students: ¥5,000 (includes guide, insurance, and materials)

【Capacity】
15 people

【Guide】
Mori no Field Gakusha staff

【What to bring】
Drink, light/flashlight, light snack, change of shoes

【Notes】
Mori no Field Gakusha website: https://morinomanabiya168.wixsite.com/website
location: 集合 飯能市立博物館前（国際興業バス「OH!!!・天覧山下」または「市民会館・博物館」下車徒歩3分)
summary: What's the light floating in early summer? ~Firefly watching in the evening satoyama~
